response:
text  
for each element in the array, calculate the absolute difference between the element and all other elements in the array, and sum them up. for elements at index 0, 3, 6, etc., calculate the square of the difference instead of the absolute difference.  

highlighted section  
for the first element in the array, calculate the sum of absolute differences between the first element and all other elements in the array. for the second element, calculate the sum of absolute differences between the second element and all other elements in the array. for the third element, calculate the sum of square differences between the third element and all other elements in the array.  

highlighted section  
for the fourth element in the array, calculate the sum of absolute differences between the fourth element and all other elements in the array. for the fifth element, calculate the sum of absolute differences between the fifth element and all other elements in the array. for the sixth element, calculate the sum of square differences between the sixth element and all other elements in the array.  

paragraph  
the algorithm works by iterating through each element in the array and calculating the required sum based on the index. for elements at indices that are multiples of 3, the square of the difference is used instead of the absolute difference. the sum is computed for each element and stored in the result array.  

paragraph  
the approach ensures that the calculation is efficient even for large arrays. the use of absolute differences and square differences allows the algorithm to handle the problem requirements accurately. the result array is built by applying the appropriate calculation for each element based on its index.
